mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of instances when the unemployment threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with accelerating inflation indicates demand-pull price pressures that force monetary tightening expectations, producing negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when retail sales growth exceeds 2% and CPI growth exceeds 2.5% simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly declines in industrial production reflect immediate supply-side shocks that trigger short-term risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Industrial production drops of greater than 1% fail to generate negative 5-day forward returns in greater than 50% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Payroll increases of greater than 150k consistently coincide with negative 20-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts dropping below 500k units signals a severe contraction in construction demand and related supply chains, driving negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 65% of months when housing starts breach the 500k threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates paired with high inflation erode real yields and signal impending policy tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combination of FEDFUNDS below 1.0% and CPIAUCSL growth above 2.0% fails to predict negative returns with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 3.0% confirms strong economic expansion that supports corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 3.0% coincides with negative 60-day forward returns in greater than 55% of observations.
